one_line_definition: Controlled low-speed abrasive sizing and surface-finishing process using expandable bonded-abrasive stones that simultaneously rotate and reciprocate inside a bore
Controlled low-speed abrasive sizing and surface-finishing process using expandable bonded-abrasive stones that simultaneously rotate and reciprocate inside a bore
Characteristic angled groove pattern on a honed bore surface produced by the combination of rotational and reciprocating stone motion; essential for piston/bore tribology
Characteristic angled groove pattern on a honed bore surface produced by the combination of rotational and reciprocating stone motion
Two-step honing process: rough honing for stock removal followed by plateau/finish honing to remove peaks and create a flat-topped, valley-retaining surface texture
Two-step honing process creating a surface with smooth plateaus for load bearing and deep valleys for oil retention

aliases_seen: Triboconditioning®, mechanochemical surface finishing, in-manufacture running-in
one_line_definition: Mechanochemical process using hard non-abrasive WC ledges and a synthetic process fluid to create a plateau surface, low-friction tribofilm, and compressive subsurface stresses without abrasive removal
Honing variant using fine grit (320–1200), very short oscillating strokes (1–4 mm), high frequency (300–2500 cpm), and light pressure on a rotating workpiece; achieves Ra < 0.1 µm
ISO 13565-2 parameter quantifying the height of protruding peaks above the core roughness zone; target of finish honing
ISO 13565-2 parameter for protruding peak height above the core roughness zone
ISO 13565-2 parameter quantifying depth of valleys below the core roughness zone; must be ≥ 0.6 µm for adequate oil retention (per rule of thumb)
ISO 13565-2 parameter for valley depth below the core roughness zone; indicator of lubrication retention capacity
ISO 13565-2 parameter for the depth of the core (kernel) region of the bearing ratio curve; reflects working contact zone roughness
ISO 13565-2 bearing ratio at which the surface transitions from peaks into the core zone
ISO 13565-2 bearing ratio at which the surface transitions from the core zone into valleys
ISO 13565-3 probability-model parameter for the RMS roughness of the plateau portion of a plateau-honed surface
ISO 13565-3 probability-model parameter for the RMS roughness of the valley portion of a plateau-honed surface
ISO 13565-3 probability-model parameter marking the bearing ratio at the transition between plateau and valley textures
Mean absolute deviation of the surface profile from the mean line; standard primary roughness parameter
Mean absolute deviation of the surface profile; biased toward plateau level in plateau-honed surfaces
Average of peak-to-valley height across multiple sampling lengths; sensitive to deep valleys and pull-outs
Mean peak-to-valley roughness height; sensitive to valley depth in plateau-honed surfaces
tp; fraction of material at a given depth level of the profile
Cumulative material ratio as a function of depth; fundamental curve for Rk and plateau-hone parameterization
Bonded abrasive stick (alumina, SiC, cBN, or diamond in vitrified, resinoid, or metal bond) used in a honing tool
Preferred abrasive type for honing cast iron, carbide, ceramics, chrome, and glass
Metal-bonded diamond stone used for extremely hard and brittle materials
Superabrasive preferred for honing hardened steels, Inconel, Stellite, and other tough alloys
Superabrasive for carbon/alloy steels; metal-bonded (grade B)
Iron-base or other metallic coatings applied to aluminum engine bores to replace iron liners; require specialized multistep honing processes
In-process size control technique using pneumatic signals to measure bore diameter during or after honing
Hybrid process combining electrochemical dissolution with abrasive honing to improve material removal rate while retaining crosshatch texture
Hybrid process combining honing with simultaneous electrodeposition to salvage or build up out-of-tolerance bore surfaces
Honing performed with the engine head bolted in place (torque plate simulating it) to pre-distort the bore so it becomes round under operating clamping loads
Honing performed at elevated temperature to account for thermal distortion of engine bores under operating conditions
Specialized honing kinematics producing extremely smooth plateaus to minimize friction and ring wear
Surface measurement instrument in which only the stylus tip contacts the surface, providing true profile including waviness; preferred for plateau hone measurement
Surface measurement instrument using a radiused skid as a datum; attenuates waviness and can introduce artefact depressions
Profile filtering method resistant to outlier peaks/valleys; recommended over traditional Gaussian filter for plateau-honed surfaces
Statistical measure of how well a process meets specification limits; bore honing targets have risen from Cpk 1.33 to 2.0+
Friction and wear interactions between piston rings and cylinder bore surface; governed by bore texture, ring pack design, and lubricant viscosity
Process fluid providing lubricity and cooling during honing; formulated with EP/AW additives; critical to abrasive life and surface quality
Process fluid providing lubricity and chip clearance; choice affects stock removal rate and surface integrity
